figures.caption: Fig. 1: Proposed method of the RAW image compressing pipeline in combination with a newly generated bubble dataset to determine image visibility on-capsule.
Fig. 6: Distribution of the images with a pathology label of the Galar dataset.
Fig. 7: Demonstrator’s runtime and accuracy analysis.
Fig. 8: Simulation of energy consumption of the 80 Galar studies with different CR Threshold and fps reduction combinations.
Fig. 2: Example images from Kvasir-Caspule and Galar dataset (top) and the corresponding images with detected bubbles (bottom).
Fig. 4: (a) Comparision of two Kvasir-Capsule’s luminal finding groups (normal clean mucosa and reduced mucosal view) and (b) the CR of Galar’s technical label group.
Fig. 3: Relation between the CR of the Bayer images and the bubble coverage with images of (a) Kvasir-Capsule and (b) Galar (small intestine only).
